entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A warm-season cereal crop [Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R. Br.] that is comparatively more tolerant to salinity.

entities.name: SOIL SALINITY
entities.description: A major crop production constraint affecting approximately 77 million ha of arable land worldwide.

entities.name: SALINITY TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand saline conditions, showing wide genetic variability in pearl millet.

entities.name: BIOMASS YIELD
entities.description: The total biological yield under saline pot conditions, used as an effective selection criterion for salinity tolerance.

entities.name: SALINITY TOLERANCE INDEX
entities.description: A metric used to evaluate and select for salinity tolerance and biomass productivity.

entities.name: POT CULTURE
entities.description: A screening method used to evaluate yield performance of germplasm accessions under saline conditions.

entities.name: FORAGE YIELD
entities.description: The potential yield of forage in pearl millet germplasm.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The potential yield of grain in pearl millet germplasm.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A region where yield potential under saline field situations was evaluated.

entities.name: WEST ASIA
entities.description: A region where yield potential under saline field situations was evaluated.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, which initiated a project to improve salinity tolerance in pearl millet.

entities.name: ICBA
entities.description: An organization collaborating with ICRISAT to screen pearl millet germplasm for salinity tolerance.

entities.name: GENETIC VARIABILITY
entities.description: The range of genetic differences for salinity tolerance reported in pearl millet.
